user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator invokes the real task or parallel command and reaches one declared fail-closed execution engine whose accepted flags govern the run.
rationale: Architecture review #108 confirmed that the tested internal single-task Run engine is not the command's production path, cmdRunTask duplicates orchestration and discards --base, and parallel execution can fall into a retired static-worker path when router construction fails. This slice resolves #27 at the owning command boundary and extends #52's fail-closed principle to release routing.
in_scope: Choose one production single-task engine and make cmd/sworn a thin option/configuration adapter to it; delete or production-wire the duplicate path so no production-dead orchestrator remains.
Thread --base from CLI parse through branch creation and gated merge and reject an unknown or non-ancestor base before model dispatch.
Require the committed-state router for production parallel runs and return a typed startup error when board, status, or router construction cannot establish the next role.
Keep the retired static iteration path available only to explicitly named compatibility tests or remove it when its final test value is migrated.
Centralise model/role resolution and option validation so dry-run and live command construction use the same engine inputs.
Add an assembled command guard proving task and parallel modes each dispatch at least one owning engine through cmd/sworn.
out_of_scope: Cancellation and restart semantics are S03-cancellation-recovery.
Parallel dry-run and loop max-turn behavior remain owned by their planned slices in 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates.
Provider capability selection and model registry expansion remain outside this release.
Fixing every state.Read validation path remains tracked by #52; this slice fails closed at the production routing boundary.

effort_complexity.rationale: Removing a duplicated production path changes command construction, task setup, routing, flags, and tests while preserving both CLI modes.

risks.description: Consolidating engines regresses one-off task creation or legacy command output.
risks.mitigation: Capture current supported task gestures as command-level fixtures and compare release artefacts, branch base, verdict, and exit code through the chosen engine.

risks.description: A compatibility switch silently re-enables static parallel routing in production.
risks.mitigation: Make compatibility injection test-only or an explicit non-production option and add an architecture rule rejecting production construction of the static path.

risks.description: Valid flags remain parsed but unused after consolidation.
risks.mitigation: A flag-consumption table maps every accepted run flag to an engine option and command tests use deliberately non-default values.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN sworn run --task is invoked THE SYSTEM SHALL execute the single declared task engine used by its direct engine tests (C-01, N-01; #27).
WHEN sworn run --task accepts --base <ref> THE SYSTEM SHALL create and merge the task branch relative to that exact validated ref (C-01, N-01; #27).
IF production parallel router construction fails THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero before any worker dispatch (C-01, N-01; #52).
WHEN a later sibling has malformed or unreadable committed status THE SYSTEM SHALL return an oracle error instead of selecting an implementation role (C-01, N-01; #52).
THE SYSTEM SHALL expose no production call site for legacy static parallel iteration (C-01, N-01).
WHEN supported run flags are set to non-default values THE SYSTEM SHALL pass each value to the owning engine option observed by the cmd/sworn integration test (N-01).
